answers: Start ice skating: rent skates, tie them snug, hold the wall first, then practice marching steps on ice.
Tips: bend knees (stay low), don't lean back, take small steps, and practice falling safely (to the side, not backward).
Balance is key — start with penguin walk, then gliding, then stopping (snowplow stop). Progress gradually.
Ice skating is great exercise and fun for all ages. Indoor rinks available year-round in most cities.
